description:
You are an edge-computing engineer who has just collected diagnostic logs from three IoT devices.  
    The logs are already copied into the directory  
        /home/user/iot_logs/  
    as the files  
        device01.log  
        device02.log  
        device03.log  

    1. Scan every “*.log” file in /home/user/iot_logs for lines that contain the string “ERROR”.  
    2. From each matching line, extract the third white-space-separated field (this field is the error-code and always starts with an uppercase “E” followed by digits, e.g. E105).  
    3. Count how many times each distinct error-code appears across *all* log files.  
    4. Create a new tab-separated summary file at  
        /home/user/iot_logs/error_summary.tsv  

       The file **must** follow the exact format below so automated grading can validate it:

       • The very first line is a header and must read exactly (case-sensitive):  
            ErrorCode<TAB>Count  

       • Each subsequent line contains one distinct error-code, followed by a tab character, followed by its total count.  
       • Sort the lines primarily by descending count and, in the case of a tie, by ascending (lexicographical) error-code.  
       • Do not include any extra columns, spaces, or commentary. End the file with a single trailing newline.

       Example layout (for illustration only – real numbers come from the actual log contents):

           ErrorCode<TAB>Count
           E999<TAB>5
           E101<TAB>3
           E050<TAB>1

    A concise set of 3–4 standard GNU/Linux shell commands (e.g., grep/awk/sort/uniq) is sufficient to complete the task, but you may use any approach available in a standard Debian/Ubuntu terminal.

    When you have finished, you can print the contents of /home/user/iot_logs/error_summary.tsv to the console so I can quickly verify it.
truth:
The testing harness will pre-create the following directory structure and files before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/iot_logs/device01.log
       ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │[2023-07-20 12:00:00] INFO  Sensor reading: 23.4               │
       │[2023-07-20 12:01:00] ERROR SENSOR_TIMEOUT E105 Connection lost│
       │[2023-07-20 12:02:00] WARN  Retry attempt                       │
       │[2023-07-20 12:03:00] ERROR LOW_BATTERY E201 Battery level 5%  │
       │[2023-07-20 12:04:00] INFO  Sensor reading: 24.1               │
       └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    2. /home/user/iot_logs/device02.log
       ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │[2023-07-20 12:10:00] INFO  Boot                               │
       │[2023-07-20 12:11:00] ERROR SENSOR_TIMEOUT E105 Connection lost│
       │[2023-07-20 12:12:00] ERROR OVERHEAT E301 Temperature 85C      │
       │[2023-07-20 12:13:00] ERROR SENSOR_TIMEOUT E105 Connection lost│
       │[2023-07-20 12:14:00] INFO  Sensor reading: 22                 │
       └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    3. /home/user/iot_logs/device03.log
       ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │[2023-07-20 12:20:00] ERROR LOW_BATTERY E201 Battery level 4%  │
       │[2023-07-20 12:21:00] ERROR OVERHEAT E301 Temperature 87C      │
       │[2023-07-20 12:22:00] WARN  Retry                              │
       │[2023-07-20 12:23:00] ERROR LOW_BATTERY E201 Battery level 3%  │
       └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    The correct /home/user/iot_logs/error_summary.tsv **after** the agent finishes should contain exactly (including the final newline):

        ErrorCode<TAB>Count
        E105<TAB>3
        E201<TAB>3
        E301<TAB>2
        (newline)

    Where <TAB> represents a single ASCII 0x09 tab character.  
    No extra spaces or blank lines are allowed.

    File ownership: user:user (uid/gid 1000:1000)  
    File permissions: 0644 for all provided files; the summary file must be writable by the user (the default umask is fine).

    The grading script will:

      • Verify that /home/user/iot_logs/error_summary.tsv exists.  
      • Confirm the header and line ordering comply with the spec.  
      • Diff the file’s contents against the exact block above (byte for byte).  
      • Optionally run grep/awk commands to double-check counts.
name: task_000000_b0c2660e
